de.uka.ilkd.key.util.pp

Class Layouter.StreamToken

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.util.pp.Layouter.StreamToken
  • Direct Known Subclasses:
    Layouter.CloseBlockToken, Layouter.IndentationToken, Layouter.MarkToken, Layouter.SizeCalculatingToken, Layouter.StringToken
    Enclosing class:
    Layouter

    private abstract class Layouter.StreamToken
extends Object
    A stream token.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Modifier Constructor and Description
      private StreamToken() 

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      (package private) int followingSize()
      Return the `section' size.
      (package private) boolean followingSizeKnown()
      Returns whether the followingSize is already known.
      (package private) abstract void print()
      Send this token to the Printer Layouter.out.
      (package private) void setEnd()
      Indicate that the corresponding next BreakToken or CloseBlockToken has been encountered.
      (package private) void setInfiniteSize()
      Indicate that the followingSize is guaranteed to be larger than the line width, and that it can thus be set to some large value.
      (package private) abstract int size()
      Return the size of this token if the block is not broken.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • StreamToken

        private StreamToken()

    • Method Detail

      • print

        abstract void print()
             throws IOException
        Send this token to the Printer Layouter.out.
        Throws:
        IOException

      • size

        abstract int size()
        Return the size of this token if the block is not broken.

      • followingSize

        int followingSize()
        Return the `section' size. For an OpenBlockToken, this is the size of the whole block, if it is not broken. For a BreakToken, it is the size of the material up to the next corresponding BreakToken or CloseBlockToken. Otherwise it is the same as size(). This might only be known after several more tokens have been read. If the value is guaranteed to be larger than what fits on a line, some large value might be returned instead of the precise size.

      • followingSizeKnown

        boolean followingSizeKnown()
        Returns whether the followingSize is already known. That is the case if either a corresponding next BreakToken or CloseBlockToken has been encountered, or if the material is known not to fit on a line.

      • setEnd

        void setEnd()
        Indicate that the corresponding next BreakToken or CloseBlockToken has been encountered. After this, followingSizeKnown() will return the correct value.

      • setInfiniteSize

        void setInfiniteSize()
        Indicate that the followingSize is guaranteed to be larger than the line width, and that it can thus be set to some large value.
